title: SCHC malformed-packet (declared vs actual length) behavior diverges three ways
description: Same malformed input, three outcomes: C lichen_schc_compress hard-validates IPv6 payload length == actual and UDP length == actual (schc.c:554-577, enforced :1514-1517) returning SCHC_ERR_NO_MATCHING_RULE (error, NO output, no 255 fallback; enshrined by tests main.c:287-339). Python headers.py:202 only checks len(raw) < 40+payload_length: declared>actual -> no profile matches -> rule 255 fallback; declared<actual -> UdpDatagram.from_bytes raises UdpError (uncaught, not a fallback) or silently TRUNCATES the tail (headers.py:210). Rust: no declared-length validation anywhere (codec.rs:269-323, headers.rs:308-327) - compresses actual bytes. Wire-presence AND wire-content divergence. DECISION NEEDED: the wire contract for malformed input (C's strictness is safest but creates asymmetric-send vs Python's 255 fallback).
